questions.question: In an aquatic ecosystem, the pyramid of biomass is often inverted. The best explanation for this is:
options: Aquatic producers photosynthesize less efficiently than terrestrial plants
Small but rapidly reproducing phytoplankton support a larger biomass of zooplankton and fish
Water reduces the energy transfer efficiency between trophic levels below 10%
Decomposers in water are more active, reducing standing biomass of producers continuously
questions.explanation: Phytoplankton have a very fast reproduction rate — their biomass at any instant is low but turnover is high. The zooplankton and fish they support have greater standing biomass, inverting the usual pattern despite the 10% energy rule still holding.

questions.question: Which of the following is the most accurate description of a Biosphere Reserve compared to a National Park?
options: A Biosphere Reserve prohibits all human activity, whereas National Parks allow limited tourism
A Biosphere Reserve has a zoned structure (core, buffer, transition) allowing graduated human use; a National Park prohibits all human habitation and resource use
Biosphere Reserves are declared under the Wildlife Protection Act; National Parks are declared under the Environment Protection Act
A Biosphere Reserve is specific to marine ecosystems; National Parks apply only to terrestrial areas
questions.explanation: Biosphere Reserves have three zones: the core zone (fully protected), buffer zone (limited research and tourism), and transition zone (sustainable human use and settlement). National Parks have stricter human exclusion — no grazing, no habitation, no resource extraction.

questions.question: Among the greenhouse gases, methane (CH₄) is considered more potent than CO₂ because:
options: Methane persists in the atmosphere for over 500 years, far longer than CO₂
Methane has a much higher global warming potential per molecule over a 100-year period
Methane reacts with ozone directly, destroying the ozone layer faster than CO₂
Methane is entirely produced by human industrial activities, unlike CO₂ which has natural sources
questions.explanation: CH₄ has a GWP of approximately 28–36 times that of CO₂ over 100 years. However, CO₂ persists in the atmosphere much longer. CH₄ degrades in roughly 12 years. Both have natural and human sources — paddy fields, cattle, and landfills are major CH₄ emitters.
